description:
A biological researcher has asked for help automating the first step in curating four small pilot datasets that already live on disk.  
    Your job is to write ONE short Bourne-shell script (5–10 commands long) that performs the actions listed below and then execute it once so that the required artefacts really exist on the filesystem.  
  
    Initial file-tree you will find on the system  
        /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A/sample1.csv  
        /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A/sample2.csv  
        /home/user/data/raw/dataset_B/sample1.csv  
        /home/user/data/raw/dataset_B/sample2.csv  
  
        (All four files are tiny text files and are already present before you start.)  
  
    What the automation must do  
    1. Create the directory /home/user/data/processed if it does not yet exist.  
    2. Copy every *.csv found anywhere under /home/user/data/raw/ into /home/user/data/processed/ while
       • flattening the directory hierarchy and  
       • renaming the files to the pattern  
              <DATASETNAME>_<FILENAME>.csv  
         For example the input file “…/raw/dataset_A/sample1.csv” must become  
              /home/user/data/processed/dataset_A_sample1.csv  
    3. Gzip each copied file in place using the “-n” switch so that the gzip payload is 100 % reproducible.
       After this step every file in /home/user/data/processed/ must have the extension “.csv.gz”.
    4. Build a one-line-per-file manifest named  
           /home/user/data/processed/manifest_datasets.csv  
       that has exactly five (5) lines total: one heading line plus one data line per compressed file.
       • Line-1 (the header) MUST be spelled exactly:  
             original_path,new_path,md5  
       • Each subsequent line is a comma-separated triplet:  
             <FULL ORIGINAL PATH>,<FULL NEW PATH>,<MD5 CHECKSUM OF THE COMPRESSED FILE>  
         Example (spacing only for illustration):  
             /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A/sample1.csv,/home/user/data/processed/dataset_A_sample1.csv.gz,0fb151c7f9b0e5…  
       • The MD5 column must contain the 32-character lowercase hexadecimal produced by “md5sum” (or equivalent) of the **compressed** file.  
       • The lines may appear in any order, but the header must be the first line.  
    5. Save the script itself at /home/user/scripts/organize_datasets.sh and make it executable (chmod + x).  
  
    Hints & constraints  
    • Only standard GNU command-line tools that exist in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu installation may be used.  
    • The whole job can be done with roughly 6-8 simple commands (mkdir, find/cp, gzip, md5sum, etc.).  
    • Remember to **run** the script once you have finished writing it – the automated grader looks for the actual files, not just the script.  
    • Do not hard-code absolute paths inside the manifest; use exactly the paths given above so the grader finds a perfect 1-to-1 match.  
  
    Validation criteria used by the grader  
    • /home/user/scripts/organize_datasets.sh exists, is executable, and contains 5–10 non-blank command lines.  
    • /home/user/data/processed/ contains exactly four *.csv.gz files named  
          dataset_A_sample1.csv.gz  
          dataset_A_sample2.csv.gz  
          dataset_B_sample1.csv.gz  
          dataset_B_sample2.csv.gz  
    • /home/user/data/processed/manifest_datasets.csv exists, is machine-readable CSV, has 5 lines, the header is correct, and every data line:  
          – points to the correct original and new paths, and  
          – the MD5 column matches the checksum of its corresponding *.csv.gz file.  
    • All compressed files are valid gzip streams (magic bytes 1F 8B) and were created with the “-n” flag (zero timestamp).
truth:
Pre-existing files and their exact contents before the agent starts  
  
      /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A/sample1.csv
        gene_id,value
        A1,5
        A2,7
  
      /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A/sample2.csv
        gene_id,value
        A3,2
        A4,9
  
      /home/user/data/raw/dataset_B/sample1.csv
        gene_id,value
        B1,4
        B2,1
  
      /home/user/data/raw/dataset_B/sample2.csv
        gene_id,value
        B3,6
        B4,3
  
  
    What the grader will verify AFTER the agent finishes  
  
    1. Script file
       • Path: /home/user/scripts/organize_datasets.sh  
       • Permission: 0755  
       • Line count (non-blank, non-comment): between 5 and 10  
    2. Processed directory contents  
       • Files present:  
            /home/user/data/processed/dataset_A_sample1.csv.gz  
            /home/user/data/processed/dataset_A_sample2.csv.gz  
            /home/user/data/processed/dataset_B_sample1.csv.gz  
            /home/user/data/processed/dataset_B_sample2.csv.gz  
       • Each is a gzip file whose uncompressed payload is byte-for-byte identical to the respective raw CSV above.  
       • No uncompressed *.csv files should remain in /home/user/data/processed.  
    3. Manifest file  
       • Path: /home/user/data/processed/manifest_datasets.csv  
       • Total lines: 5 (1 header + 4 data lines)  
       • Header must match exactly: “original_path,new_path,md5\n”  
       • For every raw CSV listed under /home/user/data/raw/, there is one row whose first two columns are the canonical paths shown in the task, and whose third column is a 32-hex-character MD5 that equals  
               md5sum /home/user/data/processed/<name>.csv.gz | cut -d' ' -f1  
         (The grader recalculates, so a correct checksum value—not any specific literal—is required.)
